key_insight: Detonation-sprayed AlCuFe quasicrystalline coatings oxidize slowly at 450°C while transforming into a more stable multiphase microstructure that preserves hardening over time.
engineering_relevance: The coating offers a promising high-temperature protective barrier for TC4 titanium alloys, improving oxidation resistance and maintaining mechanical stability during long-term exposure.
method: Fabricated AlCuFe quasicrystalline coatings by detonation spraying (DS)
Aged/oxidized coatings in air at 450°C for up to 250 h
Tracked oxidation kinetics, phase evolution, and microhardness over time
evidence_signal: The oxidation followed a parabolic law with kp = 1.83 × 10^-22 mg²·cm^-4·h^-1, while microhardness increased from 491 HV0.2 to 665.2 HV0.2 after 250 h.
watch_item: The coating’s long-term performance may depend on controlling Al depletion and phase-boundary evolution, which could vary with service temperature, atmosphere, and spray quality.
paper_title: High-temperature oxidation kinetics and microstructural evolution of detonation-sprayed AlCuFe quasicrystalline coatings
